title: primula elatior subsp. ruprechtii
intro: Primula elatior subsp. ruprechtii, also known as the large cowslip or large primrose, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant actions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, soothing digestive problems, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of Primula elatior subsp. ruprechtii can be found in the form of infusions, tinctures, and teas, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
